div.p: the beasts giving glory to God,1 and understood them. God who made the beasts could understand every language spoken by them. The four beasts2 were four of the most noble animals that had filled the measure of their creation,3 and had been saved from other worlds, because they were perfect: they were like angels in their sphere. We are not told where they came from, and I do not know; but they were seen and heard by John praising and glorifying God.4

The popular religionists of the day tell us, forsooth, that the beasts spoken of in the Revelation represent kingdoms. Very well, on the same principle we can say that the twenty-four elders spoken of represent beasts; for they are all spoken of at the same time, and are represented as all uniting in the same acts of praise and devotion.5

This learned interpretation is all as flat as a pancake! “What do you use such vulgar expressions for, being a prophet?” Because the old women understand it—they make pancakes. Deacon Homespun said the earth was flat as a pancake, and ridiculed the science which proved to the contrary. The whole argument is flat, and I don’t know of anything better to represent it. The world is full of technicalities and misrepresentation, which I calculate to overthrow, and speak of things as they actually exist.

Again, there is no revelation to prove that things do not exist in heaven as I have set forth, nor yet to show that the beasts meant anything but beasts; and we never can comprehend the things of God and of heaven,6 but by revelation.7 We may spiritualize and express opinions to all eternity; but that is no authority.
div.div: Elders to Preach Repentance and Let Mysteries Alone
div.p: Oh, ye elders of Israel,8 hearken to my voice;9 and when you are sent into the world to preach,10 tell those things you are sent to tell; preach and cry aloud,11 “Repent ye, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand;12 repent and believe the Gospel.” Declare the first principles, and let mysteries alone,13 lest ye be overthrown. Never meddle with the visions of beasts and subjects you do not understand. Elder Brown, when you go to Palmyra, say nothing about the four beasts, but preach those things the Lord has told you to preach about—repentance and baptism for the remission of sins.14
